---
title: "4.16 Recursion"
subtitle: "Part 3: Tracing Recursive Methods"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit4-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## Call Stack

### Recursive calls wait for smaller calls to finish

![Cup stack analogy](../../assets/4-data-collections/4-16-recursion/cupStack.jpg){fig-align="center" width="36%"}

Each recursive call has its own parameter values and waits until the call it made returns.

## Code and Stack

### Trace one call frame at a time

![Code for call stack](../../assets/4-data-collections/4-16-recursion/codeForCallStack.png){fig-align="center" width="48%"}

When tracing, write each call with its argument value. Then work backward as return values are produced.

## Error Call Stack

### Stack overflow means calls did not stop

![Error call stack](../../assets/4-data-collections/4-16-recursion/errorCallStack.png){fig-align="center" width="48%"}

Common causes:

::: {.tight-list}
- no base case
- base case can never be reached
- recursive call uses the same argument again
:::

## Call Tree

### Some recursion branches

![Call tree](../../assets/4-data-collections/4-16-recursion/callTree.png){fig-align="center" width="45%"}

Branching recursion creates more than one recursive call per method call. Trace small inputs first.

## Tracing Challenge

### Fill in call and return values

For:

```java
public static int mystery(int x)
{
    if (x == 0)
    {
        return 0;
    }
    return x + mystery(x - 1);
}
```

Trace `mystery(3)`: `3 + 2 + 1 + 0 = 6`.

## Classroom Check

### A complete answer should include

::: {.tight-list}
- trace recursive calls with separate argument values
- explain how the call stack grows and shrinks
- identify stack overflow as uncontrolled recursion
- distinguish call expansion from returned values
- trace small inputs before generalizing a recursive pattern
:::

## End

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)
